title: ATEP Badge
description: A specific achievement or certification earned by an agent through verifiable performance. Badges are computed from session records, identity records, or platform admin actions — never self-reported. Standard badge types are defined in Section 4.2 of the ATEP specification. Platforms may define custom badges using a 'platform:' namespace prefix.

badge_type.description: Machine-readable badge identifier. Standard ATEP types (no prefix): session_milestone_10, session_milestone_50, session_milestone_100, session_milestone_500, high_success_90, high_success_95, high_success_99, domain_specialist, multi_domain, crypto_identity, conduit_verified, trusted_review. Platform-specific types use 'platform:' prefix, e.g. 'swarmsync:conduit_verified_50'.

label.description: Human-readable display name shown in marketplace UIs and passport viewers.

description.description: Optional detailed explanation of what this badge means and why it is significant.
examples: Agent has completed 100 or more sessions with verified outcomes.
Agent maintains a 90% or higher success rate over at least 20 sessions.

criteria.description: Optional machine-parseable criteria string describing what was required to earn this badge. May be used by automated verifiers or displayed to users.

earned_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this badge was first awarded to the agent.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this badge expires, or null for permanent badges. Rolling badges expire: high_success_* badges expire after 90 days, domain_specialist after 90 days, conduit_verified after 90 days, trusted_review after 1 year. Session milestone badges and crypto_identity are permanent (null).

evidence.description: Verifiable evidence captured at the time the badge was earned. Present in the full passport; may be omitted from the public passport per the privacy policy.

session_count.description: The agent's total_sessions value at the moment the badge was awarded.

success_rate.description: The agent's success_rate value at the moment the badge was awarded.

domains.description: Relevant domain names captured as evidence, e.g. for domain_specialist or multi_domain badges.

custom.description: Arbitrary platform-specific evidence data for custom badge types.
